scan_limitations.note: These findings are based on automated static/security scanning of the repository contents only. Automated tools can produce false positives, so findings should be validated before any remediation decisions are made.

attack_vectors: Unsafe repository code patterns can turn into injection, auth bypass, or insecure deployment behavior once the code is built and released.

key_findings.title: Static analysis surfaced code-level security weaknesses
key_findings.description: 1 confirmed repository finding was surfaced outside dependency alerts.
key_findings.impact: Unsafe defaults, insecure configuration patterns, or risky code constructs can become exploitable behavior once the code is built and deployed.
key_findings.remediation: Review the confirmed code-level findings in priority order, fix the affected code paths, and re-scan before shipping.

key_findings.title: Some repository signals still require manual validation
key_findings.description: 3 findings landed in the needs-review bucket. These deserve engineering validation rather than immediate dismissal.
key_findings.impact: Unvalidated findings can either hide real exposure if dismissed too quickly or waste engineering time if they are left unresolved without context.
key_findings.remediation: Assign an engineer to validate each needs-review finding against runtime usage, repository history, and deployment context, then either promote it to a confirmed issue or document why it is benign.

key_findings.title: Dedicated malicious-code review is not enabled in this scan
key_findings.description: This repository analysis currently covers secrets, dependency vulnerabilities, and unsafe code patterns. It does not yet run a dedicated malicious-code detector for implants, trojans, cryptominers, or stealth exfiltration logic.
key_findings.impact: A clean repository vulnerability result should not be interpreted as proof that the repository is free of malicious behavior, because that detector coverage is not active yet.
key_findings.remediation: Keep this limitation explicit in the repository report and add dedicated malicious-code detectors in the next scanning phase.

malicious_code.summary: Dedicated malicious-code detection is not enabled yet. This report currently reflects repository security vulnerabilities and code-safety signals only.
malicious_code.nextStep: Next step: add a malicious-code detector so repository scans can evaluate both exploitability and hostile code presence in the same report.

ai_analysis.estimated_improvement_score: Fixing the highest-priority repository issues could improve the repository score from 92/100 to approximately 100/100. Address confirmed critical and high-severity findings first, then validate ambiguous matches before closing them.

security_findings.message: Project has 1 package file(s) that should be checked for vulnerabilities
security_findings.recommendation: Run the full dependency vulnerability scan to check for known CVEs. Regularly update dependencies with tools like Dependabot or Renovate.

security_findings.details: Regular expression denial of service in Pydantic < 2.4.0, < 1.10.13 allows remote attackers to cause denial of service via a crafted email string.
security_findings.recommendation: Update pydantic to the latest version. See GHSA-mr82-8j83-vxmv for details.

security_findings.details: NULL Pointer Dereference allows attackers to cause a denial of service (or application crash). This only applies when lxml is used together with libxml2 2.9.10 through 2.9.14. libxml2 2.9.9 and earlier are not affected. It allows triggering crashes through forged input data, given a vulnerable code sequence in the application. The vulnerability is caused by the iterwalk function (also used by the canonicalize function). Such code shouldn't be in wide-spread use, given that parsing + iterwalk would usually be replaced with the more efficient iterparse function. However, an XML converter that serialises to C14N would also be vulnerable, for example, and there are legitimate use cases for this code sequence. If untrusted input is received (also remotely) and processed via iterwalk function, a crash can be triggered.
security_findings.recommendation: Update lxml to the latest version. See GHSA-wrxv-2j5q-m38w for details.

security_findings.details: NULL Pointer Dereference allows attackers to cause a denial of service (or application crash). This only applies when lxml is used together with libxml2 2.9.10 through 2.9.14. libxml2 2.9.9 and earlier are not affected. It allows triggering crashes through forged input data, given a vulnerable code sequence in the application. The vulnerability is caused by the iterwalk function (also used by the canonicalize function). Such code shouldn't be in wide-spread use, given that parsing + iterwalk would usually be replaced with the more efficient iterparse function. However, an XML converter that serialises to C14N would also be vulnerable, for example, and there are legitimate use cases for this code sequence. If untrusted input is received (also remotely) and processed via iterwalk function, a crash can be triggered.
security_findings.recommendation: Update lxml to the latest version. See PYSEC-2022-230 for details.

Update lxml to the latest version. See PYSEC-2022-230 for details.

trust_posture_findings.title: Missing: LICENSE file
trust_posture_findings.message: No LICENSE file — unclear legal terms for use
trust_posture_findings.remediation: Add a LICENSE file. Choose an appropriate license at choosealicense.com.

trust_posture_findings.title: Missing: SECURITY.md or security policy
trust_posture_findings.message: No SECURITY.md — no clear way to report vulnerabilities
trust_posture_findings.remediation: Add a SECURITY.md with vulnerability disclosure instructions.

trust_posture_findings.title: Missing: CI/CD configuration
trust_posture_findings.message: No CI/CD configuration found — code changes may not be automatically tested
trust_posture_findings.remediation: Set up CI/CD (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.) to automatically test and validate changes.

trust_posture_findings.title: Missing: Dependency lockfile
trust_posture_findings.message: No dependency lockfile — builds may use different versions, making supply chain attacks easier
trust_posture_findings.remediation: Commit your lockfile (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, etc.) to pin dependency versions.

trust_posture_findings.title: Missing: CONTRIBUTING guide
trust_posture_findings.message: No CONTRIBUTING file — contribution process is unclear
trust_posture_findings.remediation: Add a CONTRIBUTING.md with guidelines for contributing to the project.
